channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

Mumen rider is right, the cyberpunk guy has the true count.

market cap?

>>61918519
what a pleasure. when you're dead

>what a pleasure
The phrase is "with pleasure" you ESL

61925435

61927063

n

l

61927377

61927483

crapto is dead

>Day 1650
Here's your 3/01: >>61922023
>Day 1651

>Day 1652

61932185

61932537

item.title: >BTC? you're just betting that a bigger retard will pay you more than you did why aren't people as angry about this conc… — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>BTC? you're just betting that a bigger retard will pay you more than you did why aren't people as angry about this conc…

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:24 UTC

OP

>BTC? you're just betting that a bigger retard will pay you more than you did
why aren't people as angry about this concept when the question is about gold? because cryptos have given too much "underserved" wealth to some people?

Early replies (first 3)

61936302
>>61936177
At least BTC serves an actual function. 95% of the bullion that gets traded around to spergs will never ever be smelted down and used for its conductive properties.
BTC is a currency, and you're still using it as one even if you're just saving with it.

61936315
>>61936302
>At least BTC serves an actual function.
>says this and compares ponzi fake ultrafiat "money" to GOLD
biggest fucktard I've seen on /biz/ this whole week possibly

61936320
>>61936315
my original point is that gold as an investment is motivated excactly the same as BTC, it's just more conservative and tested. BTC is just gold on steroids, the fundamental idea isn't far off.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61936323
>>61936315
>ultrafiat
But enough about gold, which needs IOUs to function as a "currency".
Crypto won. Metaltards stay eternally seething.

61936395
>>61936177
gold has intrinsic value, we make things out of gold purely because it looks pretty. If modern civilization collapsed tomorrow I could still trade my gold for goods and services like we have done for thousands of years. My btc, not so much.

61936416
>>61936395
gold's practical use explains 5% of its market value. besides that fraction, all the points about perceived value apply the same to bitcoin. the latter is obviously younger being a child of the internet age.

>le societal collapse
you got your gold physically with you right now? good for you then. i know i won't be betting on that outcome myself.

61936421
>>61936323
But you can't get btc physical?

61936438
>>61936416
the point isn't to pour all your net worth into gold but to have a safety net. My point is unlike bitcoin you can't become the greater fool buy buying physical gold.

61936444
>>61936421
Better, you get it in it's energy form.

61936469
>>61936315
He’s literally right though

Refute him without sounding mad.

61936479
>>61936438
If your pour all of your wealth into gold you actually brick yourself. You lose 5% right off the bat, put yourself in major risk in the event of a flood or catastrophe, and now are at the mercy of local dealers and spot price when you need to buy.
Even the spergs who go all in on BTC can find vendors that accept it, rapidly convert it to fiat, store it effortlessly, use payment processors that quickswap to fiat, or even get loans for collateral.
>inb4 you sperg out because i mention fiat missing the point entirely

61936512
>>61936479
>the point isn't to pour all your net worth into gold
>the point isn't to pour all your net worth into gold
>the point isn't to pour all your net worth into gold
reading is hard

61936529
>>61936512
Almost like BTC can be used as a currency and gold can't

61936536
>>61936529
this has nothing to do with the argument. Just say you misread what I typed.

61936566
>>61936438
>My point is unlike bitcoin you can't become the greater fool buy buying physical gold.
Of course you can. Don't be silly.

61936574
>>61936536
Why would I use something that no one wants or trades as an emergency fund?
>yes good sir can I please have some good
>here's 1 oz of a shiny rock
>...what do you mean you don't want it!
>ITS FUCKING CONDUCTIVE!
Like nigga ur argument is so dumb that canned corn mogs gold by the same metric
There's better ways to be a doomsdaytard, but we're here for financial solutions assuming there isn't total global nuclear war

61936576
>>61936302
You do realize there is only a limited supply of gold right? You think refineries don’t exist for a reason.

61936605
>>61936576
No, I actually thought gold was infinite. This changes everything.

61936642
>>61936574
this has nothing to do with the argument. Just say you misread what I typed.

61936655
>>61936642
>the point isn't to pour all your net worth into gold but to have a safety net.
>safety net
I did tho, ur just dumb

61936664
>me: the point isn't to pour all your net worth into gold
>you: here's why putting all your net worth into gold is bad
there is nothing more I can do for you

61936686
>>61936664
>me: the point isn't to pour all your net worth into gold
You can me this point about anything, yet that doesn't mean that you should invest in that.
For example: the point isn't to pour all your net worth into bonds
So should you hold bonds?

61936694
>>61936664
I addressed your original concern. Your entire argument is predicated on BTC having no fiat valuation, and gold is somehow divorced from the consequences of its fiat valuation. Your argument is ontologically invalid, and I'm not even sure what that word means, but I think its the right one to use.

61936781
>>61936769
except gold is legitimately a shitty investment

>>61930922

Can't even keep the count consistent, much less the goal.
Total fraud.

>>61928272
You're gonna keep up this cope even when it drops below 50k aren't you?

61932188

61932574

61932618

61932664

61933339
day 2 of snailbumping every day until the ’log is nothing but snailposting

61934029
>>61932156

61934320

61934670

61935142

61935380

item.title: This wagie has escaped — 28 replies — 88.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
This wagie has escaped

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:17 UTC

OP

I did it anons. I escaped the wagie cage, work offered me a redundancy thinking i would say no and ask to be redployed in another shitty role.
The HR roastie was visibly shaken. "But anon, the market is tough right now!" After 15 years i have paid off all debt and saved plus will be 200k after tax redundancy.

After selling my house, this will give me enough to retire in SEA. Fuck waging.

Early replies (first 3)

61933494
>>61933477
kek

61933581
>>61933477
The Light abandons no man

61933627
>>61933477
>retire in SEA
ehhhhhhhhhh

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61933725
>>61933627
I know i know anon. But it's the only viable option. Everything else is over priced or over run.
Where else can you live a good life on $1500/month when you're notw some retard with free housing from gibz

61933844
>>61933725
1500 per month on 200k is very reckless, unless you have a solid investment strategy like investing in third world bonds that yield over 10 percent annually

61933862
>>61933844
No i will sell my house and have just over 1 mil so will be fine.
This wagie is never going back in the cage

61933866
>>61933862
make sure to visit before committing to long term plans

61933873
>>61933866
It can't be worse than here

61933885
>>61933477
>The HR roastie was visibly shaken.
>called their bluff
absolutely based, anon. good work

61933899
>>61933873
It can.

61933939
>>61933899
Anon I'm starting to suspect you're either a white woman, a redditor or an asian man ass blasted about white boys mogging them at home.

61934421
>>61933899
it really can't. retiring in a LCOL area in america means you're surrounded by niggers with shit weather or you're surrounded by mayo monkey meth addicts with shit weather. living in a tropical third world country and occasionally getting some jungle gook pussy is infinitely superior.

61934436
>>61933939
fuck it, good luck to you buddy, stick to the plan, make it happen by eoy.

61934459
>>61933627
I swear there is an entire campaign op to discourage young men from doing this
>”Youll never fit in!!”
Yeah no shit not there to fit in
>everyone there will just use you for money!
Everyone here uses me for money
>You don’t speak the language!
I’m not trying to be Thai/Viet/Cambodian I’m trying no to live cheap by the beach in a touristy area where all the people I need to interact with are expats or have some working knowledge of English anyways if the don’t I can’t pay them off
>FINE YOURE A FUCKING LOSER ANYWAYS JUST STAY HERE YOU CREEP!
Don’t care

61934548
>>61933477
>quick! wagie has escaped his cagie!
>use your business and finance knowledge to re-trap him!

61934549
>>61933477
SEA might be on fire soon bro, careful there. What Taiwan kicks off Singapore might start lighting up like Dubai

61935462
>>61933477
You sound like a two digits IQ idealistic faggot but I admire your balls.
t. 44 years old NEET self-sustained god that has been neeting since his early twenties

61935470
I have like 2m and still feel poor

61935590
>>61933477
Fuck you cock sucker motherfucker AAAAGGGGGHHHHHHHH

61935647
>>61933725
In Eastern Europe? Fucking retard you can buy a $30k commie apartment and live with $800 per month and your propriety will also grow in value over time and I'm not talking some shithole like Moldova where having $500 in your pocket makes you rich.
Why the fuck are you paying $1500 per month to live in SEA and live with browns? That will turn into $2000 soon then $2500 and so on.

61935688
>>61935647
where in eastern europe?

61935716
>>61935647
Do they really let foreigners buy in those places?
Will they give me citizenship?
Are all those countries full of sociopathic emotionless corrupt people like Russians?

61935721
>>61933477
Congrats on the escape. I hope I can too but for now I’m happy some anon has freed themselves.

61935822
>>61933866
best advice in this thread
>>61933899
Check out a few different places around the world before you decide to commit to just one.
With over a million you can easily dump it into dividend/income focused ETFs and retire to a modest life pretty much anymore you want. SEA is selling yourself short.

61935846
>>61935716
>noooo people aren't smiling for no reason all the time, they're literally insane
You're not fit to live with slavs, stick to Latin America

61935869
>>61935647
lol dipshit, does all your knowledge of russia come from /pol/ and fox jews?
Every intelligent russian with money is fleeing that shithole and telling people in the west about how fucking horrible and utter shit it was, meanwhile you have turbo virgins who spend all their time crying on the internet and sucking trump's dick trying to convince you it's some white paradise.
I hope you migrate over there and get raped by an arab or central asian mongoloid.

61936723
You will love it... till you don't know what to do next. And then you'll start a bar (and usually fail like many farangs), find that you are out of money and you'll be worried what else to do
till you will be back to work.

item.title: S&P500 PE ratio — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
S&P500 PE ratio

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:31 UTC

OP

A return to mean would require a 60% drop.
Corrections often overcorrect. So... let's say 80% drop.

Early replies (first 3)

61934160
NOOOOO your historical record breaking profitable companies can't be highly valued because... they just CAN'T ok??!?

61936674
>>61934133
Explain in 33 words why? Why should it return to the 1880-1980 mean?

61936687
it can't return to the mean because that would require the us dollar supply to return to the mean. Its mathematically impossible now.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61936695
>>61934133
why dont we make it a clean 100%?

61936698
>>61936695
This is not a %, it can go beyond 100.

61936713
>>61934133
Everybody knows that fiat is dying, is the music chairs game in reverse.

61936727
>>61936687
no
you are a retard or a bot
you did not consider that both P and E are denominated in $

61936746
>>61936727
Did you know that the average PE ratio during previous centuries was in the single digits? Why didn't 1800-1900 mean revert back to 1700 levels? Why should 2000 mean revert back to 1900 levels?

61936764
>>61934133
>drop
>in the era of even faker and gayer money printer shenanigans

61936768
>>61936746
What about this? S&P500 vs US M2 supply

61936776
Or when comparing S&P500 total return to bond market total return

61936777
>>61934133
looks like it's going to 100 first

61936821
>>61936768
M2 has nothing to do with PE ratio.
Do global total stock market instead of SP500.
America may just be overvalued still (or maybe not).

item.title: >losing 50% of your investment in BTC makes you a greater fool >losing 50% of your investment in silver doesn't because… — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>losing 50% of your investment in BTC makes you a greater fool >losing 50% of your investment in silver doesn't because…

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:35 UTC

OP

>losing 50% of your investment in BTC makes you a greater fool
>losing 50% of your investment in silver doesn't because its conductive and shiny

Early replies (first 3)

61936801
Wait, what? Silver has been constantly rallying at least over $82 since the infamous drop from $120 on January 30th. What the actual fuck are you talking about?

61936810
>>61936801
silver fuddies can't do math

61936812
>>61936810
I'm just saying this chart is fucking wrong. It never dropped below the number shown there.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61936822
>>61936801
>>61936810
>>61936812
silverbaggies are so low IQ lmfao
>but its a REAAAL ROCK
>THE FIAT VALUATION DOESN'T HECKIN MATTER!
>it will go to a bajillion one day tho and I'll buy your sister as le sex slave with le mercury dime :^)

61936828
>>61936822
I bet you don't have any physical silver you dumb fucking broad.

61936831
>>61936822
>THE FIAT VALUATION DOESN'T HECKIN MATTER!
>it will go to a bajillion one day tho and I'll buy your sister as le sex slave with le mercury dime :^)
Unironically yes.

61936835
>>61936828
I bought fiat with mine @$103 and oz
can you remind me of the current price?

61936836
The most illegal market manipulation in the history of investing was required to keep it from going straight up endlessly. Buttcoins do these kinds of things organically every day

61936841
>>61936835
You are an ABSOLUTE dumbass. Thanks for letting me know. Enjoy bankrupcty.

61936844
>>61936836
>ITS REAAAAAAALLLL
>BUT DA JOOOEEEES MAKE THE VALUE NOt REAAALLLL
LOLLLLL!

item.title: Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1 — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:23 UTC

OP

Day 1 of snailposting every day until JASMY is at $1

Early replies (first 3)

61934693
jasmy

61935114
>>61934344
10m jasmy daimyo here
2 words
Apple partnership
I've been stacking since 2023

61935154
based

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61935550
ohh jasmy

61935609
>>61935597
uhhhhhh what is this

61935646
>>61935609
>Jasmy now has a compliant domestic payment rail through its collaboration with Aplix Corporation, which was licensed by Japan’s Financial Services Agency to issue electronic money. This doesn’t confirm that JPYD is launched or that it is a bank-backed stablecoin, but it establishes the legal infrastructure needed to build and deploy regulated digital payments (wallets, merchant payments, local government programs, etc.) that could later integrate blockchain settlement. In short: Jasmy gains regulatory groundwork for real payment infrastructure in Japan, but the exact structure of JPYD is still unknown.

61935684
>>61935646
sounds interesting especially considering mcap is low rn
would this actually go to the token though? I thought the token was supposed to be for iot? I don't even remember anymore it's been years since I sold.

61936250
JASMY stays ORGASMY
also buy cheese

61936773
JASMY

item.title: WHAT THE FUCK — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WHAT THE FUCK

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:36 UTC

OP

Everything, EVERYTHING is pumping except for this piece of SHIT

Early replies (first 3)

61934647
Trust the President. The coin is literally digital gold

61935071
What do you think will happen once Trump leaves office? How will you get new buyers?

61935085
>>61935071
If something awful happened to trump in minecraft this solana shit coin would probably pump.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61935089
>>61935085
And where's WHACKD now?

61935680
>>61934645
80% unlocked

61935906
>>61934645
KEEP BUYING! Only libtards think it's a scam. This is gonna pump to godly levels and libcucks/trannies are gonna be on suicide watch

61936837
>>61935089
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LJVzRQA2NPI

61936845
>>61936837
one more transact and ill have your ass whackd!

item.title: TSLA probably still has a 3-4x in it — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
TSLA probably still has a 3-4x in it

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:21 UTC

OP

What would it take to get there?

Early replies (first 3)

61936400
>>61936120
a few funny posts on x

61936470
>>61936120
Only thing that might do it is their humanoid robots. But China might as well be ahead of that too.

61936556
>>61936120
Buy a stock trading at 300 times earnings because it will be tading at 1000 times earnings.
Genius.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61936638
>>61936556
They will find a way to make more money. The earnings wont stay where they are now

61936758
Tesla is currently grossly overpriced for a car company but pivoting to cute customizable robot maids would be an easy 10x

item.title: What is the best way to profit off of 'the president of peace'? — 7 replies — 13.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is the best way to profit off of 'the president of peace'?

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:05 UTC

OP

What is the best way to profit off of 'the president of peace'?

We all know that Israel and the US's governments are in bed together, so much so that it makes more sense to think of these two countries as extensions of each other rather than individual countries. The war in Iran is 100% going to escalate further.

How do I actually make money off of this? I am already knee deep in PLTR. Is now the time to buy Lockheed Martin and Boeing? What other defense stocks are good to buy?

Early replies (first 3)

61934184
>he's even considering buying anything other than AI stocks
why, they're the ones that will pump, none of this will make sense and you'll blame the market for being irrational again

61934247
>>61934184
They're all overvalued. I kind of think PLTR is overvalued but that has a high likelihood of pumping

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61934341
>>61934295
This dumb fuck just destroyed the GOP and the global right wing for at least two decades didn't he.

61934446
>>61934341
one can dream

61934450
>>61934295
Private insurers won't take on the risk so the American Taxpayer will do it. Just wipe your ass with all these dollars were printing.

61936645
>>61934341
Yes he did. In fact he destroyed it not for two decades, but forever.
A few of us foresaw this, but almost nobody listened.
2024 was a nightmare for criticizing Trump. Always had retards saying we are jews, controlled opposition, or spreading disinformation. Online and IRL.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 3 replies — 8.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-03-04 21:14 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.
